content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian and Bengali.You are given an integer NNN. Output a permutation of values from 111 to NNN that satisfies the following condition:


gcd⁡([1+A1,2+A2,3+A3,…,N+AN])>1\gcd([1+A_1,2+A_2,3+A_3,\ldots,N+A_N]) > 1gcd([1+A1​,2+A2​,3+A3​,…,N+AN​])>1
It can be proven that a solution always exists. If there are multiple permutations that can satisfy the condition, then output any one of them.

As a reminder,


A permutation of values from 111 to NNN is an array containing integers from 111 to NNN in any order but each of them appearing exactly once.
GCD stands for Greatest Common Divisor. The greatest common divisor of a sequence is the largest integer ddd such that all the numbers in sequence are divisible by ddd. For more information, refer to here.

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on one line a permutation of values from 111 to NNN which satisfies the above condition.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 5000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with N integers – a permutation of 1…N such that gcd(i + A_i) for i = 1..N is greater than 1. Numbers are separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Positions 1,2,3,4 receive 3,4,1,2 respectively. All sums are even: [4,6,4,6]; their GCD is 2 > 1.

solution.approach: For the GCD to be > 1 it is enough to make every term i + A_i even, because then the GCD is at least 2. i + A_i is even iff i and A_i have the same parity. Therefore we only have to place odd numbers on odd positions and even numbers on even positions. The set of odd positions is exactly the set of odd numbers {1,3,5,…} and similarly for evens. We can obtain a valid permutation by rotating each parity class by one position: for the list of odd positions p1,p2,…,pk assign A_{p_j}=p_{j+1} (with the last receiving the first), and do the same for evens. This works for any N (including N=2 where each class has size 1). The construction is O(N) per test case and uses O(N) extra space for the answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) overall.
